seoTitle: How to Customize the Zendesk Help Center Design
metaDescription: Learn how to customize the Zendesk Help Center using the no-code theme designer or the CSS code editor, then preview and publish your changes.
intro: A Help Center that still looks like the default template doesn't build trust with customers browsing for answers. Zendesk Guide's Customize Design panel lets you brand your self-service portal without writing code, or dig into the theme's CSS if you need finer control. This guide covers both paths.

steps.heading: Open the design panel
steps.body: In Knowledge Admin, click the Customize Design sidebar icon and select Themes.

steps.heading: Use the no-code theme editor
steps.body: Click Customize Theme to launch the visual brand options panel for quick styling changes.

steps.heading: Upload brand assets
steps.body: Add your corporate logo, favicon, and adjust global typography fonts to match your brand.

steps.heading: Set colors and layout options
steps.body: Match your main palette colors and toggle homepage section column display settings.

steps.heading: Switch to the code editor if needed
steps.body: Click Edit Code to open the local CSS stylesheet and template files for more advanced customization.

steps.heading: Preview and publish
steps.body: Use the sandbox preview tool to check layouts on multiple screen sizes, then click Publish and set it as your live theme.

faq.question: Do I need to know CSS to customize the Help Center?
faq.answer: No, the no-code theme designer covers most common branding needs like colors, logos, and typography without any code.

faq.question: Can I undo a design change after publishing?
faq.answer: Downloading a rollback backup ZIP archive before making major changes lets you restore a previous version if needed.

faq.question: Will my changes affect the underlying articles?
faq.answer: No, design customization applies global style changes without touching your published article content.

faq.question: How do I check if my theme works on mobile?
faq.answer: The interactive sandbox preview tool lets you verify layouts across different screen sizes before publishing.
